title: PIZDA
tagline: Policy Intelligence for Zero-Trust Decision Automation
body_md:
## Overview

PIZDA is an AI governance control plane that intercepts every LLM request to enforce EU AI Act, GDPR, and data sovereignty policies in real-time. It sits between applications and LLM providers as a transparent policy enforcement layer.

## Problem

As LLM adoption accelerates, organizations face growing regulatory pressure from the EU AI Act and GDPR. Existing solutions either block LLM use entirely or rely on post-hoc auditing — neither is suitable for production agentic systems.

## Approach

PIZDA implements a zero-trust model: every LLM request is intercepted, evaluated against OPA/Rego policy rules, and classified using an XGBoost model trained on regulatory violation patterns. SHAP values provide explainability for every policy decision.

## Technical Architecture

- **Policy Engine**: OPA/Rego for declarative policy rules (EU AI Act, GDPR, data sovereignty)
- **ML Classifier**: XGBoost + SHAP for risk scoring and explanation
- **Proxy Layer**: Go-based reverse proxy with sub-10ms overhead
- **Dashboard**: Next.js admin console for policy management and audit trails
- **Storage**: Postgres (audit logs) + Redis (rate limiting, caching)
- **Deployment**: Docker Compose, production-ready

## Outcomes

- Real-time policy enforcement with <10ms added latency
- Full audit trail for regulatory compliance
- Explainable risk scores for every LLM interaction
- Support for 15+ policy rules covering EU AI Act requirements

title: PQC-TLS Authentication System
tagline: Post-Quantum Secure API Layer replacing RSA-JWT
body_md:
## Overview

A complete post-quantum authentication stack that replaces RSA-JWT with CRYSTALS-Dilithium (NIST Level-3) digital signatures, integrated into a containerized FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Redis backend.

## Problem

RSA and ECDSA digital signatures are vulnerable to Shor's algorithm on quantum computers. Organizations need to migrate authentication infrastructure to NIST-standardized PQC algorithms before quantum computers become practical.

## Approach

Implemented CRYSTALS-Dilithium signature scheme via liboqs-python as a drop-in replacement for JWT-based authentication. Built comprehensive benchmarking infrastructure to compare PQC vs classical signature schemes under load.

## Technical Architecture

- **Auth**: CRYSTALS-Dilithium (NIST Level-3) via liboqs-python
- **Backend**: FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Redis (Docker Compose)
- **Key Management**: PQC key generation, secure storage, automated rotation
- **Dashboard**: React + WebSocket for live authentication event feed
- **RBAC**: Per-identity risk scoring and policy editor

## Outcomes

- Benchmarked Dilithium vs RSA-2048 and ECDSA across 10,000 concurrent requests
- Demonstrated NIST Level-3 security with acceptable performance overhead
- End-to-end demo-ready system delivered in 2-week cycle

title: NyayaAI
body_md:
## Overview

A 16-agent hierarchical pipeline that automates structured extraction of legal issues, arguments, and precedents from 50–200 page Indian court judgments into structured JSON.

## Problem

Indian court judgments are lengthy, unstructured legal documents. Manual extraction of key legal issues, arguments, and precedents is time-consuming and error-prone.

## Approach

Built a hierarchical multi-agent system where specialized agents handle different aspects of legal document analysis. Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables real-time inter-agent communication without database intermediaries.

## Agent Architecture

- **Extraction Layer**: Document parser, issue identifier, argument extractor, precedent finder
- **Intelligence Layer**: Judge profiler, counter-argument predictor, outcome predictor
- **Validation Layer**: Source-span verification, cross-reference validator, consistency checker
- **Orchestration**: GenW.AI Agent Builder with MCP

## Outcomes

- 85%+ structured output accuracy on test judgments
- Significant latency reduction vs database-intermediary approaches
- Handles 50–200 page judgments end-to-end

title: Finveritas
tagline: Explainable AI Financial Statement Analysis Platform
body_md:
## Overview

A constraint-aware, offline-first multi-agent financial analysis platform aligned with Basel III Pillar-2 principles for explainable internal credit risk assessment.

## Problem

Financial institutions need explainable AI for credit risk assessment under Basel III Pillar-2. Black-box LLM outputs are unsuitable for regulatory environments requiring auditability.

## Approach

Designed specialized financial analysis agents with deterministic output pipelines and cross-reference validation. Eliminated black-box reasoning in favor of auditable chains.

## Technical Architecture

- **Agents**: Revenue Stability, Liquidity Monitoring, Balance Sheet Risk, Cross-Reference Validator
- **RAG**: ChromaDB vector store with Bloomberg PDF parsing
- **Reasoning**: Deterministic, auditable reasoning chains (LangChain)
- **UI**: Bloomberg-style terminal dashboard in Streamlit

## Outcomes

- Regulatory alignment with Basel III Pillar-2 requirements
- Fully explainable, auditable AI decisions for credit risk assessment
- Interactive drill-down financial investigation dashboard
